name: Discourse
h1: Build AI Agents That Work with Discourse
metaTitle: Build Discourse AI Agents with DocsBot Skill Builder
metaDescription: Create DocsBot AI agents that use Discourse for support lookups, community updates, handoffs, moderation workflows, and internal team assistance.
intro: Discourse powers community discussions, support forums, and team collaboration, which makes it a strong system for AI agents that need live conversation context. With DocsBot Skill Builder, you can describe the Discourse workflow you want in plain language, and DocsBot designs, secures, tests, and deploys a reusable Skill for your support bot or internal AI assistant. The result is an AI agent that can answer questions and take approved actions inside Discourse instead of only telling people what to do next.
supportUseCases: Let a customer-facing DocsBot agent look up relevant Discourse topics, solved discussions, or category guidance and answer customers with current community knowledge.
Have the agent create a Discourse topic for a customer issue when a new case needs community follow-up, product feedback review, or public troubleshooting.
Enable the agent to summarize a long Discourse thread into a clear customer reply so users get fast answers without reading every post.
Use the agent to prepare a handoff by collecting the customer problem, linking matching Discourse discussions, and drafting an escalation summary for human support.
Allow the agent to update approved topic details, apply tags, or route conversations into the right Discourse category when support workflows require structured follow-up.
internalUseCases: Employees can chat with a private DocsBot agent to find past Discourse discussions, moderation decisions, feature requests, or troubleshooting posts without manually searching the forum.
Support and community teams can ask the agent to summarize active Discourse topics, highlight repeated customer issues, and prepare internal briefings for product or success teams.
Community managers can use the agent in Slack or Microsoft Teams to draft new Discourse announcements, organize categories, and follow standard moderation workflows with approval boundaries.
Operations teams can chat with the agent to retrieve Discourse activity context, compile discussion trends, and generate handoff notes for cross-functional follow-up.
Internal teams can ask the agent to research what the community has already discussed on a topic and turn those findings into a reusable response, FAQ draft, or escalation summary.
whatYouCanBuild: A support agent that searches Discourse topics and returns the best answer with discussion links.
An AI workflow that creates new Discourse topics from customer issues that need community follow-up.
A moderation assistant that tags, categorizes, and summarizes posts within approved action limits.
An internal research agent that finds similar discussions and prepares product feedback summaries.
A handoff Skill that turns long community threads into concise support escalation notes.
A team assistant that drafts Discourse announcements, release posts, and community update summaries.

howItWorks.title: Describe the outcome
howItWorks.description: You tell Skill Builder what you want the agent to do in Discourse, such as finding forum answers, creating topics, tagging discussions, or preparing handoffs, and it translates that goal into a reusable Skill design.

howItWorks.title: Skill Builder secures it
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder identifies the required connection details, defines secure authentication and configuration values, keeps secrets out of chats and Skill source, proxies credentials through approved runtime controls, and scopes access to the minimum Discourse actions and records needed.

howItWorks.title: Actions get safe boundaries
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder designs the workflow with clear limits around who can use it, which topics or categories it can access, what updates are allowed, and where approvals are required before sensitive actions are taken.

howItWorks.title: Testing agent exercises scenarios
howItWorks.description: DocsBot automatically runs imagined customer support and internal team conversations against the Skill, finds bugs, weak instructions, permission gaps, or workflow issues, and feeds those results into an automated improvement loop.

howItWorks.title: Publish to the right agent
howItWorks.description: Once the Skill is improved and approved, Skill Builder helps deploy it to your customer-facing DocsBot chat widget, helpdesk-connected support agent, or private internal assistant in chat, Slack, or Microsoft Teams.

faq.question: How can I build an AI agent that works with Discourse?
faq.answer: With DocsBot Skill Builder, you describe the Discourse workflow you want in plain language, and it creates a Skill that can search discussions, summarize threads, create records, or trigger approved follow-up actions. Skill Builder also handles secure connection setup, scoped permissions, testing, and deployment.

faq.question: Can an AI support agent use Discourse content to answer customer questions?
faq.answer: Yes, a DocsBot support agent can use Discourse as a live knowledge source to find relevant discussions, solved topics, and community guidance while replying to customers in chat or helpdesk tickets. It can also summarize long threads and prepare human handoffs when a case needs escalation.

faq.question: What Discourse workflows can an internal AI assistant automate for employees?
faq.answer: An internal DocsBot agent can help employees search community history, summarize product feedback, review moderation context, draft announcements, and follow standard operating procedures around community management. The workflow can include bounded actions, approval steps, and generated summaries for other teams.

faq.question: Can DocsBot safely create or update Discourse topics through an AI agent?
faq.answer: Yes, DocsBot Skill Builder can design Skills that create topics, apply tags, organize categories, or update approved records inside Discourse with carefully scoped access. It keeps credentials protected, limits the actions the agent can take, and can add approval boundaries for sensitive changes.

faq.question: Can a DocsBot agent prepare support handoffs using Discourse discussions?
faq.answer: Yes, the agent can gather matching Discourse threads, summarize the conversation history, and draft a structured handoff for a human support or community team member. This helps teams respond faster while keeping important context attached to the escalation.

faq.question: How does setup work for a non-technical team using Discourse with DocsBot?
faq.answer: You explain the business outcome you want, and Skill Builder handles the Skill design, secure configuration, secret handling, testing, improvement, and deployment steps for you. The process is built to feel like planning an agent outcome, not managing an engineering project.

brand.title: Discourse
brand.description: Discourse is a comprehensive community platform that facilitates online communication and collaboration. It combines long-form discussion with real-time chat, offering features such as moderation controls, custom user privileges, and reporting. With over 60 official plugins, Discourse provides a unique experience for each site, making it an ideal solution for online communities, discussions, support, and teamwork.
brand.slogan: Meaningful discussions, support, and teamwork in one place
